expectations.text: actions.json exists, is valid JSON, and its 'actions' array covers every slot f, g, u, o, space
expectations.evidence: No actions.json produced. Output is the V1 format: base_prompt.txt, hold_prompts.txt (WASD movement holds), world.json with holds keyed w/a/s/d/idle. No f/g/u/o/space slots

expectations.text: every action's prompt_en equals the base prompt + ' ' + addendum_en, joined verbatim (no paraphrase)
expectations.evidence: No prompt_en fields exist anywhere in the output; hold prompts are standalone rewrites, not base+addendum concatenations

expectations.text: the space action is exactly 'The character jumps high into the air.' with label 'Jump'
expectations.evidence: No space/jump action exists; the five states are W/S/A/D movement plus idle

expectations.text: the base prompt contains an explicit motion contract tying all motion to key input (WASD / arrow-key clause with 'only while held' or 'in response to keyboard')
expectations.evidence: Base describes the puppy 'waiting for movement input' but never ties motion to keys; no WASD/arrow-key/'in response to keyboard'/'only while held' clause in the base prompt

expectations.text: the u addendum is a melee strike on a NEARBY object with visible material destruction; the o addendum is a projectile with a trajectory, a DISTANT named target, and impact aftermath
expectations.evidence: No melee or ranged actions exist at all; all states are locomotion (gallop, backpedal, arc left/right)

expectations.text: no addendum restates the camera lock or input contract (except the f subject-preservation clause), and each addendum is 1-3 sentences
expectations.evidence: No addenda in the base+addendum sense; hold prompts deliberately restate the camera offset in every state (response.md: 'camera offset... restated in every hold-prompt')

expectations.text: the base prompt uses the playable-character regime: third-person gameplay video, camera follows from behind and slightly above, and a game-style anchor (e.g. Unreal Engine 5 style)
expectations.evidence: Camera is behind/above ('one meter behind and slightly above') but base is not framed as a gameplay video and has no game-style anchor ('Bright, playful, photoreal style'); no WASD clause in base
